title: President Trump signs legislation reauthorizing SBIR
summary: President Trump signed the Small Business Innovation and Economic Security Act, reauthorizing SBIR and STTR programs through 2031 to fund early-stage space companies. The legislation also strengthens applicant screening for security purposes.

title: Washington agrees on space urgency, but not on how to deliver
summary: Washington acknowledges the urgency of addressing space industry challenges but remains divided on the best approach to resolve structural issues. Policymakers and industry leaders emphasize that financial investment alone is insufficient to overcome existing bottlenecks.

title: Q&A: Heather Pringle on what to expect from Space Symposium
summary: Heather Pringle, CEO of the Space Foundation, previews the 41st annual Space Symposium, highlighting the industry's growth in civil and national security sectors. The event aims to sustain the space industry's resurgence.

title: Phantom Space eyes edge in orbital data race with thermal deal
summary: Phantom Space has acquired a key thermal technology to bolster its position in the orbital data center market, aiming to compete with industry giants amid rising AI-driven demand. The Tucson-based satellite and rocket developer seeks to establish a vertically integrated model for orbital data services.

title: Aerospace to support industry with government furnished talent
summary: Aerospace Corp. launches a new program called Government Furnished Talent (GFT) to provide industry access to its expertise, facilities, and federally funded research and development resources. The initiative aims to accelerate the development of space capabilities by leveraging Aerospace's talent, technology, and laboratory infrastructure.

title: Spring fever pitch: Three questions to listen for this Space Symposium
summary: The 41st Space Symposium in Colorado Springs highlights optimism in the space industry, with discussions on historic moon missions, record Space Force budgets, and new valuations. Industry leaders emphasize speed, execution, and collaboration between government and private sectors.

title: The path to $50 billion in new space investment
summary: The Trump administration aims to attract $50 billion in new space investment by 2028 to support commercial space activities, including moon base establishment and LEO economy development. American companies are seen as critical to achieving this goal.

title: Amazon reveals aviation antenna as LEO inflight connectivity race intensifies
summary: Amazon has unveiled the antenna for its upcoming constellation to provide gigabit speeds to commercial aircraft, intensifying the competition in the LEO inflight connectivity market dominated by Starlink.

title: Fueling test suggests imminent debut of China’s reusable Long March 10B rocket
summary: China conducted a wet dress rehearsal for its reusable Long March 10B rocket, indicating a potential launch within weeks. This test marks a significant step toward the rocket's debut.

title: Gravitics targets 2027 flight test for ‘orbital carrier’ architecture
summary: Gravitics, a Seattle-based startup, plans to conduct a flight test in 2027 for its 'orbital carrier' architecture, which aims to pre-position spacecraft in orbit for rapid deployment.

title: NASA science faces 'very serious threat' from new White House budget, experts say
summary: NASA's science programs are at risk due to a new White House budget proposal, which experts describe as the least transparent in decades. The cuts threaten critical research and missions across the agency.

title: Huge Northrop Grumman Cygnus XL cargo ship arrives at space station
summary: The uncrewed Northrop Grumman Cygnus XL spacecraft, the S.S. Steven R. Nagel, successfully arrived at the International Space Station on April 13, delivering approximately 11,000 pounds of supplies.

title: How Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman saved the mission's moon mascot: 'It's hard not to love this little guy. I can't let Rise out of my sight'
summary: Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman saved the mission's moon mascot, Rise, which carries 5.6 million names. The mascot became a beloved symbol for the crew.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts celebrate successful return to Earth | Space photo of the day for April 13, 2026
summary: Artemis 2 astronauts Victor Glover and Christina Koch celebrated their successful return to Earth after splashdown. The event was highlighted as the Space photo of the day for April 13, 2026.

title: Artemis 2: Our favorite photos from NASA's historic moon mission
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission has released stunning photos from its historic moon mission. The images highlight key moments and details of the lunar journey.

title: 'God of Chaos' asteroid Apophis will blaze across the sky on April 13, 2029 — here's why this once-in-a-lifetime event is worth traveling for
summary: The asteroid Apophis will pass extremely close to Earth on April 13, 2029, closer than some satellites. This rare event is considered a once-in-a-lifetime stargazing opportunity visible from Europe and Africa.

title: A worst-case solar storm could knock out satellites, GPS and power grids, report warns
summary: A recent report warns that a worst-case solar storm could disrupt satellites, GPS, and power grids, potentially impacting modern society's technology. Scientists emphasize the risks posed by a once-in-a-century solar event.

title: From spa to boat party: 10 epic events for the Aug. 12, 2026, total solar eclipse in Spain and Iceland
summary: The news highlights various events planned for the total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026, in Spain and Iceland, including festivals, spa sessions, and skywatching activities.

title: 'The most special thing that will ever happen in my life': Artemis 2 astronauts describe their epic moon mission
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts have returned to Earth after their historic moon mission, struggling to articulate the profound experience. They are now beginning the process of debriefing and analyzing the mission.

title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku
summary: Super Typhoon Sinlaku is forecasted to hit the U.S. Northern Mariana Islands and Guam in mid-April 2026, posing significant risks to the region. The storm's trajectory and intensity are being closely monitored for potential impacts on infrastructure and operations.

title: NASA Invites Media to Rollout Event for Artemis III Moon Rocket Stage
summary: NASA will roll out the largest section of the SLS rocket for the Artemis III mission from its Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on April 20. This section, comprising the liquid hydrogen tank and other components, is a key part of the rocket that will launch the second crewed Artemis mission.

title: NASA Announces 32nd Annual Human Exploration Rover Challenge Winners
summary: NASA concluded its 32nd annual Human Exploration Rover Challenge with a final event on April 10-11, where student teams from around the world competed in designing, building, and testing rovers. The challenge, held at the U.S. Space & Rocket Center in Huntsville, Alabama, spanned nine months.

title: NASA Awards Data Engineering, Informatics Support Contract
summary: NASA selected Development Seed to provide research and development services for the Office of Data Science and Informatics at Marshall Space Flight Center. The contract, worth up to $76 million, starts with a phase-in period in May 2026.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4852–4858: When Data Take Their Time…
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover team faced delays in receiving data for planning sols 4852–4858, leading to creative problem-solving. The issue occurred during a planning session led by a planetary mineralogy professor.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4845-4851: Bye-Bye Boxwork, Bye-Bye
summary: The Curiosity rover's latest blog describes its exploration of boxwork terrain and its transition to a layered sulfate unit. The mission team plans to continue driving and analyzing the Martian surface.

title: NASA to Host Artemis II Crew Postflight News Conference
summary: NASA will host a postflight news conference on April 16 with the Artemis II astronauts to discuss their lunar mission. The crew includes NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Aboard USS John P. Murtha
summary: The Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar flight in over 50 years, successfully concluded with astronauts returning to Earth aboard the USS John P. Murtha. The crew included NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: NASA Night-light Imagery Tracks US Energy Transition, Global Volatility
summary: NASA's new nighttime satellite imagery reveals significant changes in global energy patterns, including increased flaring over U.S. oil and gas fields and shifts due to rural electrification and energy conservation. The findings challenge previous assumptions about artificial lighting and energy use.

title: Hubble Spies an Active Spiral
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the barred spiral galaxy IC 486, located 380 million light-years from Earth in the constellation Gemini. The galaxy's luminous swirl and ethereal light are highlighted in the new observation.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Roll out for Static Fire Testing
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have been rolled out for static fire testing as part of the Artemis program, following the successful return of Orion.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission concluding its CRS NG-23 flight.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than any previous crewed mission. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing operations at the facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station (ISS), while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. This comes as NASA's Artemis II mission approaches its halfway point.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to advance lunar exploration.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology. It highlights improvements in directional control for better mission accuracy.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team tested graphene aerogels with lasers during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025, observing significant propulsion in microgravity. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, demonstrated that light propulsion could be controlled and enhanced in space-like conditions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Lasers combined with graphene could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment simulating microgravity conditions demonstrated the potential of this technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed with capabilities spanning multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space industry applications. This innovation could enhance satellite and mission technologies.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities. The events aim to engage industry in ESA programs and gather feedback on follow-up actions.

title: Exclusive: Vast Debuts Flight Suit For Haven-1, Private Astronaut Missions
summary: Vast has unveiled a flight suit designed for its upcoming Haven-1 space station and private astronaut missions. The company aims to modernize astronaut apparel with a stylish, functional design reminiscent of cargo pants.

title: Atomic-6 Launches Orbital Data Center Marketplace
summary: Atomic-6, a composite manufacturer based in Georgia, has launched ODC.Space, a marketplace for orbital data centers, simplifying the process of securing orbital compute resources. The initiative aims to accelerate the adoption of space-based computing.

title: Sophia Space and Kepler Sign Agreement to Demo ODC Tech
summary: Sophia Space and Kepler Communications have signed an agreement to demonstrate orbital data center (ODC) technology in space. This marks Sophia Space's first opportunity to validate its ODC software in a real-world orbital environment.

title: Hawkeye 360 Files For IPO
summary: Hawkeye 360, a space-based RF data and analytics firm, has filed for an IPO to raise capital as the defense-focused space industry expands. This move follows a trend of similar companies going public to access more funding.

title: Albedo Ratchets Up the Power for its Second VLEO Flight
summary: Albedo unveiled a new VLEO-capable satellite bus designed for its second orbital mission, scheduled for 2027. The company aims to enhance power capabilities for this upcoming flight.

title: Moog’s “Tip to Tail” Contributions to the Artemis II Flight
summary: Moog has supplied over 100 components for the Artemis II mission, covering hardware from the rocket to the Orion spacecraft. The company's contributions span the entire flight system.

title: Artemis II Gives Airbus Hope For European Spaceflight
summary: The Artemis II mission features the European Service Module (ESM) built by Airbus Defence and Space, signaling potential growth for European spaceflight capabilities. This collaboration between Airbus and ESA highlights Europe's increasing role in NASA's lunar program.

title: Portal Closes $50M Series A
summary: Portal has raised $50 million in a Series A funding round led by Geodesic Capital and Mach33. The round also included investments from Booz Allen Ventures, Ark Invest, AlleyCorp, and FUSE.

title: The State of Launch 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's VP of launch discusses the competitive pressures in the space industry, highlighting how deadlines and capability demonstrations can determine winners and losers in launch contracts.

title: Vantor Unveils New Sat Classes: Vantage and Pulse
summary: Vantor introduced two new satellite classes, Vantage and Pulse, aimed at enhancing imagery resolution and revisit rate. This marks a significant advancement in the company's satellite technology portfolio.

title: Lockheed Martin Planning Next-Generation Space Dominance Demos On Orbit
summary: Lockheed Martin is self-funding two next-generation space dominance demonstrations for rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO) and command, aligning with the U.S. Defense Department's demand for orbital warfare capabilities.

title: Regulators Challenged With Keeping Up With the Cadence of Space Development
summary: Regulators struggle to keep up with the rapid pace of commercial space industry development over the past decade. They face challenges in effectively overseeing this growth while enabling innovation.

title: ThinKom Rolls Out New ThinAir Nexus Multi-Orbit, Multi-System Antenna for Airlines
summary: ThinKom introduced the ThinAir Nexus, a new multi-orbit electronically steered antenna (ESA) designed for aircraft. The California-based satellite ground-systems company unveiled the technology to enhance connectivity for airlines.

title: MDA Announces Orbital Servicing Platform
summary: MDA Space unveiled MDA Midnight, an on-orbit servicing platform for rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO), at the Space Symposium. The platform is designed to detect, identify, and counter potential threats in space.

title: Darktrace’s Research Shows New Chinese Modus Operandi
summary: Darktrace released a research report titled 'Crimson Echo' analyzing evolving cyber threats linked to Chinese-nexus activity. The report highlights new modus operandi in cyber tradecraft and associated risks.

title: Anthropic Launches Project Glasswing for Cybersecurity
summary: Anthropic has launched Project Glasswing, a cybersecurity initiative aimed at securing critical software, in collaboration with Amazon Web Services.

title: Top Space Cyber Execs Talk Increased Iranian Cyber Attacks
summary: Space companies report a rise in cyber attacks from Iran, with top CISOs from Vantor, SES, Viasat, and Telesat discussing the growing threat to the industry.

title: NSA Reveals Details of New LEO Security Report
summary: The NSA and other agencies released a new Cybersecurity Information Sheet (CSI) highlighting cybersecurity risks and mitigation strategies for Low Earth Orbit (LEO) systems. The report aims to address vulnerabilities in space-based communications and infrastructure.

title: Artemis II Crew Completes Historic Lunar Flyby Mission
summary: The Artemis II crew successfully completed a historic lunar flyby mission and splashed down in the Pacific Ocean after nearly 10 days. This marked the first crewed mission of NASA's Artemis program.

title: Emirates Connectivity Exec Explains the Airline’s Move to Starlink
summary: Emirates announced its decision to adopt Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi across its entire fleet in November 2023. The airline's connectivity executive explained the strategic move to enhance passenger connectivity.
